lessons.title: Welcome to Islam
lessons.description: Begin your beautiful journey into the faith
lessons.content:
# Welcome to Islam

Assalamu Alaikum (Peace be upon you)! Welcome to the most beautiful journey of your life.

## What is Islam?

Islam is a complete way of life, a path of peace, purpose, and connection with your Creator. The word "Islam" comes from the Arabic root "s-l-m," which means peace and submission to the will of Allah (God).

## Your New Beginning

Taking the Shahada (declaration of faith) is like being reborn. All your past mistakes are forgiven, and you start with a clean slate. This is one of the beautiful mercies of Allah.

> "Say, O My servants who have transgressed against themselves, do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah forgives all sins. Indeed, it is He who is the Forgiving, the Merciful." — Quran 39:53

## What to Expect

Over the next 90 days, we will guide you through:
- **Core beliefs** — Understanding who Allah is and what Islam teaches
- **Prayer (Salah)** — Learning how to connect with Allah five times daily
- **Quran** — Beginning your relationship with Allahs words
- **Daily habits** — Building a life of purpose and remembrance

Take your time. There is no rush. Allah brought you here at exactly the right moment.

**Welcome home. 💚**

lessons.title: The Meaning of Shahada
lessons.description: Understanding the declaration of faith
lessons.content:
# The Meaning of Shahada

The Shahada is the foundation of Islam. Let us understand its profound meaning.

## The Two Parts

**La ilaha illa Allah** — There is no god but Allah
**Muhammad rasul Allah** — Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah

## First Part: Tawheed

"La ilaha illa Allah" is a declaration of Tawheed (monotheism). It means:
- There is only ONE God worthy of worship
- Allah has no partners, children, or equals
- Only Allah can truly help us, guide us, and answer our prayers

This is liberating! We do not bow to money, fame, peoples opinions, or our own desires. We submit only to the One who created us.

## Second Part: Prophethood

"Muhammad rasul Allah" means we:
- Accept Muhammad ﷺ as the final prophet
- Follow his teachings (Sunnah)
- Love and respect him as our guide

> "Indeed in the Messenger of Allah you have a good example to follow..." — Quran 33:21

## Living the Shahada

The Shahada is not just words—it is a commitment to:
- Worship Allah alone
- Follow the Prophets example
- Live with purpose and gratitude

You have already taken this step. Now we build upon this beautiful foundation.
